13: /// Custom retriever that routes `$ref` fetches through jvl's disk cache.
14: struct CachingRetriever {

18: impl jsonschema::Retrieve for CachingRetriever {

30: /// Describes how a URL schema was resolved from cache.
31: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
32: pub enum CacheOutcome {

35:     /// Schema was not in cache and was fetched from the network.

37:     /// Disk-cache entry was stale; a re-fetch was attempted.
38:     Stale,
39:     /// Cache was explicitly bypassed via --no-cache.

41: }

43: impl CacheOutcome {
44:     pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {

62: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Error)]
63: pub enum SchemaError {
64:     #[error("Failed to read schema file '{path}': {reason}")]
65:     FileRead { path: String, reason: String },
66:     #[error("Failed to parse schema from '{path}': {reason}")]
67:     ParseError { path: String, reason: String },
68:     #[error("Failed to fetch schema from '{url}': {reason}")]
69:     FetchError { url: String, reason: String },
70:     #[error("Failed to compile schema: {0}")]
71:     CompileError(String),

73: 
74: impl SchemaError {
75:     /// The underlying reason without the schema path/URL prefix.
76:     /// Use this when the schema location is already shown in a source span.
77:     pub fn reason(&self) -> &str {
78:         match self {
79:             SchemaError::FileRead { reason, .. }
80:             | SchemaError::ParseError { reason, .. }
81:             | SchemaError::FetchError { reason, .. } => reason,
82:             SchemaError::CompileError(msg) => msg,
83:         }
84:     }
85: }

87: /// Normalize a file path for use as a cache key.
88: ///
89: /// Uses `std::fs::canonicalize` when the file exists (resolves symlinks,
90: /// case, and `.`/`..` segments). Falls back to lexical normalization
91: /// (strip `.` and `..` segments without I/O) for non-existent paths.
92: pub(crate) fn normalize_file_path(path: &Path) -> PathBuf {
93:     std::fs::canonicalize(path).unwrap_or_else(|_| normalize_lexical(path))
94: }
95: 
96: /// Strip `.` and `..` segments from an absolute path without touching the filesystem.
97: fn normalize_lexical(path: &Path) -> PathBuf {
98:     let mut components = Vec::new();
99:     for component in path.components() {
100:         match component {
101:             std::path::Component::CurDir => {}
102:             std::path::Component::ParentDir => {
103:                 components.pop();
104:             }
105:             c => components.push(c),
106:         }
107:     }
108:     components.iter().collect()
109: }
110: 
111: /// Where the schema came from.
112: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Hash, PartialEq, Eq)]
113: pub enum SchemaSource {
114:     /// A local file path (absolute).
115:     File(PathBuf),
116:     /// An HTTP/HTTPS URL.
117:     Url(String),
118: }
119: 
120: impl SchemaSource {
121:     /// Create a `File` source with a normalized path.
122:     pub fn file(path: PathBuf) -> Self {
123:         SchemaSource::File(normalize_file_path(&path))
124:     }
125: }
126: 
127: impl std::fmt::Display for SchemaSource {
128:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
129:         match self {
130:             SchemaSource::File(p) => write!(f, "{}", p.display()),
131:             SchemaSource::Url(u) => write!(f, "{u}"),
132:         }
133:     }
134: }

136: /// Resolve a `$schema` string to a SchemaSource.
137: ///
138: /// - URLs (http:// or https://) become `SchemaSource::Url`.
139: /// - Absolute paths become `SchemaSource::File`.
140: /// - Relative paths are resolved relative to `base_dir`.
141: pub fn resolve_schema_ref(schema_ref: &str, base_dir: &Path) -> SchemaSource {
142:     if schema_ref.starts_with("http://") || schema_ref.starts_with("https://") {
143:         SchemaSource::Url(schema_ref.to_string())
144:     } else {
145:         let path = Path::new(schema_ref);
146:         let abs = if path.is_absolute() {
147:             path.to_path_buf()
148:         } else {
149:             base_dir.join(path)
150:         };
151:         SchemaSource::file(abs)
152:     }
153: }
154: 
155: /// Try to resolve a schema source from the `$schema` field in a parsed JSON value.
156: ///
157: /// Returns `None` if the value has no `$schema` string field. Relative paths
158: /// in the `$schema` value are resolved against the parent directory of
159: /// `file_path`.
160: pub fn resolve_schema_from_value(
161:     parsed_value: &serde_json::Value,
162:     file_path: &Path,
163: ) -> Option<SchemaSource> {
164:     parse::extract_schema_field(parsed_value).map(|schema_ref| {
165:         let base_dir = file_path.parent().unwrap_or(Path::new("."));
166:         resolve_schema_ref(schema_ref, base_dir)
167:     })
168: }
169: 
170: /// Cache directory for fetched schemas.
171: pub fn cache_dir() -> Option<PathBuf> {
172:     dirs::cache_dir().map(|d| d.join("jvl").join("schemas"))
173: }
174: 
175: /// SHA-256 hex digest of a URL, used as cache key.
176: fn url_hash(url: &str) -> String {
177:     let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
178:     hasher.update(url.as_bytes());
179:     format!("{:x}", hasher.finalize())
180: }
181: 
182: /// Metadata for a cached schema (internal on-disk format).
183: #[derive(Debug, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize)]
184: struct CacheMeta {
185:     url: String,
186:     fetched_at: String,
187:     #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
188:     etag: Option<String>,
189: }
190: 
191: /// Information about a single cached schema entry.
192: #[derive(Debug)]
193: pub struct CachedSchemaInfo {
194:     /// The original URL of the cached schema.
195:     pub url: String,
196:     /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the schema was fetched.
197:     pub fetched_at: String,
198:     /// Size of the cached schema content in bytes.
199:     pub size: u64,
200: }
201: 
202: /// Result of listing cached schemas.
203: pub struct CacheListResult {
204:     /// Successfully read cache entries, sorted by URL.
205:     pub entries: Vec<CachedSchemaInfo>,
206:     /// Number of `.meta` files that could not be read or parsed.
207:     pub skipped: usize,
208: }
209: 
210: /// Result of clearing the cache.
211: pub enum CacheClearResult {
212:     /// Cache was cleared successfully.
213:     Cleared,
214:     /// Cache directory did not exist (already empty).
215:     AlreadyEmpty,
216: }
217: 
218: /// TTL for cached schemas: 24 hours.
219: const CACHE_TTL_SECS: i64 = 24 * 60 * 60;
220: 
221: /// HTTP request timeout.
222: const HTTP_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(30);
223: 
224: /// Load schema content from a source, using disk cache for URLs.
225: ///
226: /// Returns `(schema_json_string, warnings, cache_outcome)`.
227: /// `cache_outcome` is `None` for file-based schemas (no caching involved).
228: fn load_schema_content(
229:     source: &SchemaSource,
230:     no_cache: bool,
231: ) -> Result<(String, Vec<Warning>, Option<CacheOutcome>), SchemaError> {
232:     match source {
233:         SchemaSource::File(path) => {
234:             let content = fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| SchemaError::FileRead {
235:                 path: path.display().to_string(),
236:                 reason: e.to_string(),
237:             })?;
238:             Ok((content, vec![], None))
239:         }
240:         SchemaSource::Url(url) => load_url_schema(url, no_cache),
241:     }
242: }
243: 
244: fn load_url_schema(
245:     url: &str,
246:     no_cache: bool,
247: ) -> Result<(String, Vec<Warning>, Option<CacheOutcome>), SchemaError> {
248:     let hash = url_hash(url);
249: 
250:     if no_cache {
251:         let content = fetch_url(url)?;
252:         return Ok((content, vec![], Some(CacheOutcome::Bypassed)));
253:     }
254: 
255:     let cache_base = cache_dir();
256: 
257:     // Try to read from cache
258:     if let Some(ref base) = cache_base {
259:         let schema_path = base.join(format!("{hash}.json"));
260:         let meta_path = base.join(format!("{hash}.meta"));
261: 
262:         if schema_path.exists() {
263:             let cached_content = fs::read_to_string(&schema_path).ok();
264: 
265:             if let Some(content) = cached_content {
266:                 if is_within_ttl(&meta_path) {
267:                     return Ok((content, vec![], Some(CacheOutcome::Hit)));
268:                 }
269: 
270:                 // Stale: attempt re-fetch. Use fresh content if successful,
271:                 // fall back to stale content on failure.
272:                 match fetch_url(url) {
273:                     Ok(fresh) => {
274:                         let _ = write_cache(base, &hash, url, &fresh);
275:                         return Ok((fresh, vec![], Some(CacheOutcome::Stale)));
276:                     }
277:                     Err(_) => {
278:                         let warning = Warning {
279:                             code: "cache(stale)".into(),
280:                             message: format!(
281:                                 "Using stale cached schema for {url} (re-fetch failed)"
282:                             ),
283:                         };
284:                         return Ok((content, vec![warning], Some(CacheOutcome::Stale)));
285:                     }
286:                 }
287:             }
288:         }
289:     }
290: 
291:     // No cache hit — fetch synchronously
292:     let content = fetch_url(url)?;
293: 
294:     // Write to cache
295:     if let Some(ref base) = cache_base {
296:         let _ = write_cache(base, &hash, url, &content);
297:     }
298: 
299:     Ok((content, vec![], Some(CacheOutcome::Miss)))
300: }
301: 
302: fn is_within_ttl(meta_path: &Path) -> bool {
303:     let meta_str = fs::read_to_string(meta_path).ok();
304:     let meta = meta_str
305:         .as_deref()
306:         .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str::<CacheMeta>(s).ok());
307:     let fetched_at = meta
308:         .as_ref()
309:         .and_then(|m| m.fetched_at.parse::<jiff::Timestamp>().ok());
310:     match fetched_at {
311:         Some(ts) => ts.duration_until(jiff::Timestamp::now()).as_secs() < CACHE_TTL_SECS,
312:         None => false,
313:     }
314: }
315: 
316: static HTTP_CLIENT: OnceLock<reqwest::blocking::Client> = OnceLock::new();
317: 
318: fn get_http_client() -> &'static reqwest::blocking::Client {
319:     HTTP_CLIENT.get_or_init(|| {
320:         reqwest::blocking::Client::builder()
321:             .timeout(HTTP_TIMEOUT)
322:             .build()
323:             .expect("failed to build HTTP client")
324:     })
325: }
326: 
327: fn fetch_url(url: &str) -> Result<String, SchemaError> {
328:     let client = get_http_client();
329: 
330:     let resp = client
331:         .get(url)
332:         .send()
333:         .map_err(|e| SchemaError::FetchError {
334:             url: url.to_string(),
335:             reason: e.to_string(),
336:         })?;
337:     if !resp.status().is_success() {
338:         return Err(SchemaError::FetchError {
339:             url: url.to_string(),
340:             reason: format!("HTTP {}", resp.status()),
341:         });
342:     }
343:     resp.text().map_err(|e| SchemaError::FetchError {
344:         url: url.to_string(),
345:         reason: e.to_string(),
346:     })
347: }
348: 
349: fn write_cache(base: &Path, hash: &str, url: &str, content: &str) -> Result<(), std::io::Error> {
350:     fs::create_dir_all(base)?;
351:     let schema_path = base.join(format!("{hash}.json"));
352:     let meta_path = base.join(format!("{hash}.meta"));
353: 
354:     // Refuse to follow symlinks (defense-in-depth, consistent with clear_cache).
355:     for path in [&schema_path, &meta_path] {
356:         if let Ok(m) = fs::symlink_metadata(path)
357:             && m.file_type().is_symlink()
358:         {
359:             return Err(std::io::Error::new(
360:                 std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
361:                 format!(
362:                     "cache file is a symlink; refusing to write: {}",
363:                     path.display()
364:                 ),
365:             ));
366:         }
367:     }
368: 
369:     fs::write(&schema_path, content)?;
370:     let meta = CacheMeta {
371:         url: url.to_string(),
372:         fetched_at: jiff::Timestamp::now().to_string(),
373:         etag: None,
374:     };
375:     let meta_json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&meta).unwrap();
376:     fs::write(&meta_path, meta_json)?;
377:     Ok(())
378: }
379: 
380: /// List all cached schemas from the disk cache.
381: ///
382: /// Returns entries sorted by URL. Entries with corrupt or unreadable `.meta`
383: /// files are counted in `skipped` rather than silently ignored.
384: pub fn list_cached_schemas() -> Result<CacheListResult, std::io::Error> {
385:     let dir = cache_dir().ok_or_else(|| {
386:         std::io::Error::new(
387:             std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound,
388:             "cannot determine cache directory",
389:         )
390:     })?;
391: 
392:     let entries = match fs::read_dir(&dir) {
393:         Ok(e) => e,
394:         Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {
395:             return Ok(CacheListResult {
396:                 entries: vec![],
397:                 skipped: 0,
398:             });
399:         }
400:         Err(e) => return Err(e),
401:     };
402: 
403:     let mut infos: Vec<CachedSchemaInfo> = Vec::new();
404:     let mut skipped: usize = 0;
405: 
406:     for entry in entries {
407:         let entry = match entry {
408:             Ok(e) => e,
409:             Err(_) => {
410:                 skipped += 1;
411:                 continue;
412:             }
413:         };
414:         let path = entry.path();
415:         if path.extension().and_then(|e| e.to_str()) != Some("meta") {
416:             continue;
417:         }
418: 
419:         let content = match fs::read_to_string(&path) {
420:             Ok(c) => c,
421:             Err(_) => {
422:                 skipped += 1;
423:                 continue;
424:             }
425:         };
426:         let meta: CacheMeta = match serde_json::from_str(&content) {
427:             Ok(m) => m,
428:             Err(_) => {
429:                 skipped += 1;
430:                 continue;
431:             }
432:         };
433: 
434:         let json_path = path.with_extension("json");
435:         let size = fs::metadata(&json_path).map(|m| m.len()).unwrap_or(0);
436: 
437:         infos.push(CachedSchemaInfo {
438:             url: meta.url,
439:             fetched_at: meta.fetched_at,
440:             size,
441:         });
442:     }
443: 
444:     infos.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.url.cmp(&b.url));
445: 
446:     Ok(CacheListResult {
447:         entries: infos,
448:         skipped,
449:     })
450: }
451: 
452: /// Clear all cached schemas from the disk cache.
453: ///
454: /// Refuses to operate if the cache directory is a symlink (defense-in-depth).
455: pub fn clear_cache() -> Result<CacheClearResult, std::io::Error> {
456:     let dir = cache_dir().ok_or_else(|| {
457:         std::io::Error::new(
458:             std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound,
459:             "cannot determine cache directory",
460:         )
461:     })?;
462: 
463:     // Check for symlink before deletion (defense-in-depth).
464:     match fs::symlink_metadata(&dir) {
465:         Ok(meta) if meta.file_type().is_symlink() => {
466:             return Err(std::io::Error::new(
467:                 std::io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
468:                 "cache directory is a symlink; refusing to clear",
469:             ));
470:         }
471:         Ok(_) => {} // real directory, proceed
472:         Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => {
473:             return Ok(CacheClearResult::AlreadyEmpty);
474:         }
475:         Err(e) => return Err(e),
476:     }
477: 
478:     match fs::remove_dir_all(&dir) {
479:         Ok(()) => Ok(CacheClearResult::Cleared),
480:         Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(CacheClearResult::AlreadyEmpty),
481:         Err(e) => Err(e),
482:     }
483: }
484: 
485: /// Thread-safe cache of compiled schema validators.
486: ///
487: /// Uses per-slot `OnceLock` to ensure each schema is fetched and compiled
488: /// exactly once, even under concurrent access from multiple rayon threads.
489: #[derive(Default)]
490: pub struct SchemaCache {
491:     slots: Mutex<HashMap<SchemaSource, Arc<SchemaSlot>>>,
492: }
493: 
494: struct SchemaSlot {
495:     compiled: OnceLock<SlotResult>,
496:     /// Ensures only the initializing thread reports warnings.
497:     warnings_taken: AtomicBool,
498: }
499: 
500: struct SlotResult {
501:     validator: Result<Arc<jsonschema::Validator>, SchemaError>,
502:     schema_value: Option<Arc<serde_json::Value>>,
503:     warnings: Vec<Warning>,
504:     cache_outcome: Option<CacheOutcome>,
505: }
506: 
507: impl SchemaCache {
508:     pub fn new() -> Self {
509:         Self::default()
510:     }
511: 
512:     /// Return all `SchemaSource::File` paths currently in the cache.
513:     pub fn cached_file_paths(&self) -> Vec<PathBuf> {
514:         self.slots
515:             .lock()
516:             .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
517:             .keys()
518:             .filter_map(|source| match source {
519:                 SchemaSource::File(p) => Some(p.clone()),
520:                 _ => None,
521:             })
522:             .collect()
523:     }
524: 
525:     /// Evict a cached schema slot, forcing recompilation on the next
526:     /// [`get_or_compile`](Self::get_or_compile) call for this source.
527:     ///
528:     /// Returns `true` if the source was present in the cache.
529:     pub fn evict(&self, source: &SchemaSource) -> bool {
530:         self.slots
531:             .lock()
532:             .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
533:             .remove(source)
534:             .is_some()
535:     }
536: 
537:     /// Retrieve the raw schema JSON for a previously compiled source.
538:     ///
539:     /// Returns `None` if the source has not been compiled yet or if compilation
540:     /// failed before the schema could be parsed.
541:     pub fn get_schema_value(&self, source: &SchemaSource) -> Option<Arc<serde_json::Value>> {
542:         let slots = self.slots.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
543:         let slot = slots.get(source)?;
544:         let result = slot.compiled.get()?;
545:         result.schema_value.clone()
546:     }
547: 
548:     /// Get or compile the schema and return the raw schema JSON value.
549:     ///
550:     /// Combines [`get_or_compile`](Self::get_or_compile) and
551:     /// [`get_schema_value`](Self::get_schema_value) in a single operation.
552:     pub fn get_or_compile_with_value(
553:         &self,
554:         source: &SchemaSource,
555:         no_cache: bool,
556:     ) -> Result<Option<Arc<serde_json::Value>>, SchemaError> {
557:         let _ = self.get_or_compile(source, no_cache)?;
558:         Ok(self.get_schema_value(source))
559:     }
560: 
561:     /// Get or load+compile a schema validator.
562:     ///
563:     /// Returns `(validator, warnings, cache_outcome)`. The validator is wrapped
564:     /// in `Arc` for cheap cloning across threads. Warnings are only returned to
565:     /// the first caller (the one that triggered compilation). `cache_outcome` is
566:     /// `None` for file-based schemas or when the result was already compiled
567:     /// in-memory by another thread.
568:     pub fn get_or_compile(
569:         &self,
570:         source: &SchemaSource,
571:         no_cache: bool,
572:     ) -> Result<CompileResult, SchemaError> {
573:         let slot = {
574:             let mut slots = self.slots.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
575:             slots
576:                 .entry(source.clone())
577:                 .or_insert_with(|| {
578:                     Arc::new(SchemaSlot {
579:                         compiled: OnceLock::new(),
580:                         warnings_taken: AtomicBool::new(false),
581:                     })
582:                 })
583:                 .clone()
584:         };
585: 
586:         // OnceLock::get_or_init guarantees exactly one thread runs the closure.
587:         // Other threads calling concurrently will block until init completes.
588:         let result = slot.compiled.get_or_init(|| {
589:             let (content, warnings, cache_outcome) = match load_schema_content(source, no_cache) {
590:                 Ok(r) => r,
591:                 Err(e) => {
592:                     return SlotResult {
593:                         validator: Err(e),
594:                         schema_value: None,
595:                         warnings: vec![],
596:                         cache_outcome: None,
597:                     };
598:                 }
599:             };
600: 
601:             let schema_value: serde_json::Value = match serde_json::from_str(&content) {
602:                 Ok(v) => v,
603:                 Err(e) => {
604:                     return SlotResult {
605:                         validator: Err(SchemaError::ParseError {
606:                             path: source.to_string(),
607:                             reason: e.to_string(),
608:                         }),
609:                         schema_value: None,
610:                         warnings,
611:                         cache_outcome,
612:                     };
613:                 }
614:             };
615: 
616:             let schema_value = Arc::new(schema_value);
617: 
618:             let validator = match jsonschema::options()
619:                 .with_retriever(CachingRetriever { no_cache })
620:                 .build(&schema_value)
621:             {
622:                 Ok(v) => v,
623:                 Err(e) => {
624:                     return SlotResult {
625:                         validator: Err(SchemaError::CompileError(e.to_string())),
626:                         schema_value: Some(Arc::clone(&schema_value)),
627:                         warnings,
628:                         cache_outcome,
629:                     };
630:                 }
631:             };
632: 
633:             SlotResult {
634:                 validator: Ok(Arc::new(validator)),
635:                 schema_value: Some(schema_value),
636:                 warnings,
637:                 cache_outcome,
638:             }
639:         });
640: 
641:         // Only the first caller to reach here takes the warnings.
642:         let is_first = !slot.warnings_taken.swap(true, Ordering::Relaxed);
643:         let warnings = if is_first {
644:             result.warnings.clone()
645:         } else {
646:             vec![]
647:         };
648: 
649:         // Only the initializing thread gets the real cache outcome;
650:         // subsequent threads see None (already compiled in memory).
651:         let cache_outcome = if is_first { result.cache_outcome } else { None };
652: 
653:         match &result.validator {
654:             Ok(v) => Ok((Arc::clone(v), warnings, cache_outcome)),
655:             Err(e) => Err(e.clone()),
656:         }
657:     }
658: }
659: 
660: /// Schema annotations (title/description) for a JSON path.
661: pub struct SchemaAnnotation {
662:     pub title: Option<String>,
663:     pub description: Option<String>,
664: }
665: 
666: impl SchemaAnnotation {
667:     /// Format as Markdown for LSP hover display.
668:     ///
669:     /// Returns `None` if both `title` and `description` are `None`.
670:     pub fn to_markdown(&self) -> Option<String> {
671:         const MAX_LEN: usize = 10_000;
672:         let truncate = |s: &str| -> String {
673:             if s.len() > MAX_LEN {
674:                 let end = s.floor_char_boundary(MAX_LEN);
675:                 format!("{}...", &s[..end])
676:             } else {
677:                 s.to_string()
678:             }
679:         };
680: 
681:         match (self.title.as_deref(), self.description.as_deref()) {
682:             (Some(t), Some(d)) => Some(format!("**{}**\n\n{}", truncate(t), truncate(d))),
683:             (Some(t), None) => Some(format!("**{}**", truncate(t))),
684:             (None, Some(d)) => Some(truncate(d)),
685:             (None, None) => None,
686:         }
687:     }
688: }
689: 
690: /// Walk a raw schema `serde_json::Value` following a JSON pointer path and
691: /// return structured schema annotations (title/description).
692: ///
693: /// Handles `properties`, `items`/`prefixItems`, and fragment-only `$ref`.
694: /// Returns `None` if no `title` or `description` annotation is found.
695: pub fn lookup_schema_annotation(
696:     schema: &serde_json::Value,
697:     pointer: &[String],
698: ) -> Option<SchemaAnnotation> {
699:     let mut visited_refs: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
700:     let subschema = resolve_subschema(schema, schema, pointer, &mut visited_refs, 0)?;
701: 
702:     let title = subschema
703:         .get("title")
704:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
705:         .map(|s| s.to_string());
706:     let description = subschema
707:         .get("description")
708:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
709:         .map(|s| s.to_string());
710: 
711:     if title.is_none() && description.is_none() {
712:         return None;
713:     }
714: 
715:     Some(SchemaAnnotation { title, description })
716: }
717: 
718: /// Walk a raw schema `serde_json::Value` following a JSON pointer path and
719: /// return formatted hover content (title/description as Markdown).
720: ///
721: /// Handles `properties`, `items`/`prefixItems`, `allOf`, and fragment-only `$ref`.
722: /// Returns `None` if no `title` or `description` annotation is found.
723: pub fn lookup_hover_content(schema: &serde_json::Value, pointer: &[String]) -> Option<String> {
724:     lookup_schema_annotation(schema, pointer)?.to_markdown()
725: }
726: 
727: /// Navigate to the subschema at a JSON pointer path, following `$ref` and `allOf`.
728: ///
729: /// Public entry point that initializes cycle-detection state internally.
730: /// Returns `None` if the path cannot be resolved.
731: pub fn resolve_subschema_at_pointer<'a>(
732:     root: &'a serde_json::Value,
733:     pointer: &[String],
734: ) -> Option<&'a serde_json::Value> {
735:     let mut visited = HashSet::new();
736:     resolve_subschema(root, root, pointer, &mut visited, 0)
737: }
738: 
739: /// Maximum recursion depth for schema traversal (defense against pathological schemas).
740: const MAX_SCHEMA_DEPTH: usize = 32;
741: 
742: /// Information about a completable property from a schema.
743: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
744: pub struct PropertyInfo {
745:     pub name: String,
746:     pub required: bool,
747:     pub description: Option<String>,
748:     pub schema_type: Option<String>,
749: }
750: 
751: /// Possible value suggestions for a property.
752: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
753: pub enum ValueSuggestion {
754:     /// A value from a schema `enum` array.
755:     Enum(serde_json::Value),
756:     /// A value from a schema `const`.
757:     Const(serde_json::Value),
758:     /// Suggests `true` and `false`.
759:     Boolean,
760:     /// Suggests `null`.
761:     Null,
762: }
763: 
764: /// Collect all completable properties from a schema at the given pointer path.
765: ///
766: /// Follows `$ref` and merges `allOf` branches. Returns an empty vec if the
767: /// resolved subschema is not an object schema or the pointer cannot be resolved.
768: pub fn collect_properties(root: &serde_json::Value, pointer: &[String]) -> Vec<PropertyInfo> {
769:     let mut visited = HashSet::new();
770:     let Some(subschema) = resolve_subschema(root, root, pointer, &mut visited, 0) else {
771:         return vec![];
772:     };
773: 
774:     let mut props: Vec<PropertyInfo> = Vec::new();
775:     let mut seen_names: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
776:     collect_properties_from(root, subschema, &mut props, &mut seen_names, 0);
777:     props
778: }
779: 
780: /// Recursively collect properties from a schema, merging allOf branches.
781: fn collect_properties_from(
782:     root: &serde_json::Value,
783:     schema: &serde_json::Value,
784:     props: &mut Vec<PropertyInfo>,
785:     seen: &mut HashSet<String>,
786:     depth: usize,
787: ) {
788:     if depth > MAX_SCHEMA_DEPTH {
789:         return;
790:     }
791: 
792:     // Follow $ref.
793:     let mut visited = HashSet::new();
794:     let Some(schema) = follow_ref(root, schema, &mut visited) else {
795:         return;
796:     };
797: 
798:     // Collect required set for this schema.
799:     let required: HashSet<&str> = schema
800:         .get("required")
801:         .and_then(|v| v.as_array())
802:         .map(|arr| arr.iter().filter_map(|v| v.as_str()).collect())
803:         .unwrap_or_default();
804: 
805:     // Collect properties.
806:     if let Some(properties) = schema.get("properties").and_then(|v| v.as_object()) {
807:         for (name, prop_schema) in properties {
808:             if !seen.insert(name.clone()) {
809:                 continue; // Already seen from an earlier allOf branch.
810:             }
811: 
812:             // Follow $ref on the property schema for metadata.
813:             let mut prop_visited = HashSet::new();
814:             let resolved_prop =
815:                 follow_ref(root, prop_schema, &mut prop_visited).unwrap_or(prop_schema);
816: 
817:             let description = format_annotation(resolved_prop);
818:             let schema_type = resolved_prop
819:                 .get("type")
820:                 .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
821:                 .map(|s| s.to_string());
822: 
823:             props.push(PropertyInfo {
824:                 name: name.clone(),
825:                 required: required.contains(name.as_str()),
826:                 description,
827:                 schema_type,
828:             });
829:         }
830:     }
831: 
832:     // Recurse into allOf branches.
833:     if let Some(all_of) = schema.get("allOf").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
834:         for branch in all_of {
835:             collect_properties_from(root, branch, props, seen, depth + 1);
836:         }
837:     }
838: }
839: 
840: /// Format title and description from a schema node into a single string.
841: fn format_annotation(schema: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<String> {
842:     let title = schema.get("title").and_then(|v| v.as_str());
843:     let description = schema.get("description").and_then(|v| v.as_str());
844:     match (title, description) {
845:         (Some(t), Some(d)) => Some(format!("{t}: {d}")),
846:         (Some(t), None) => Some(t.to_string()),
847:         (None, Some(d)) => Some(d.to_string()),
848:         (None, None) => None,
849:     }
850: }
851: 
852: /// Collect possible value suggestions for a property at the given pointer path.
853: ///
854: /// Navigates to the subschema at `pointer` + `property_name`, then inspects
855: /// `enum`, `const`, and `type` to generate value suggestions.
856: pub fn collect_values(
857:     root: &serde_json::Value,
858:     pointer: &[String],
859:     property_name: &str,
860: ) -> Vec<ValueSuggestion> {
861:     let mut full_pointer: Vec<String> = pointer.to_vec();
862:     full_pointer.push(property_name.to_string());
863: 
864:     let Some(subschema) = resolve_subschema_at_pointer(root, &full_pointer) else {
865:         return vec![];
866:     };
867: 
868:     let mut suggestions = Vec::new();
869: 
870:     // Check for const.
871:     if let Some(const_val) = subschema.get("const") {
872:         suggestions.push(ValueSuggestion::Const(const_val.clone()));
873:         return suggestions;
874:     }
875: 
876:     // Check for enum.
877:     if let Some(enum_vals) = subschema.get("enum").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
878:         for val in enum_vals {
879:             suggestions.push(ValueSuggestion::Enum(val.clone()));
880:         }
881:         return suggestions;
882:     }
883: 
884:     // Check for type-based suggestions.
885:     if let Some(type_str) = subschema.get("type").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
886:         match type_str {
887:             "boolean" => suggestions.push(ValueSuggestion::Boolean),
888:             "null" => suggestions.push(ValueSuggestion::Null),
889:             _ => {}
890:         }
891:     }
892: 
893:     suggestions
894: }
895: 
896: /// Resolve the subschema at a given JSON pointer path within a schema.
897: fn resolve_subschema<'a>(
898:     root: &'a serde_json::Value,
899:     schema: &'a serde_json::Value,
900:     pointer: &[String],
901:     visited: &mut HashSet<String>,
902:     depth: usize,
903: ) -> Option<&'a serde_json::Value> {
904:     if depth > MAX_SCHEMA_DEPTH {
905:         return None;
906:     }
907: 
908:     // Follow $ref before descending.
909:     let schema = follow_ref(root, schema, visited)?;
910: 
911:     if pointer.is_empty() {
912:         return Some(schema);
913:     }
914: 
915:     let segment = &pointer[0];
916:     let rest = &pointer[1..];
917: 
918:     // Try properties.<segment>
919:     if let Some(prop_schema) = schema
920:         .get("properties")
921:         .and_then(|p| p.get(segment.as_str()))
922:     {
923:         return resolve_subschema(root, prop_schema, rest, visited, depth + 1);
924:     }
925: 
926:     // Try allOf branches.
927:     if let Some(all_of) = schema.get("allOf").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
928:         for branch in all_of {
929:             if let Some(result) = resolve_subschema(root, branch, pointer, visited, depth + 1) {
930:                 return Some(result);
931:             }
932:         }
933:     }
934: 
935:     // Try items (for array elements with numeric index).
936:     if let Ok(idx) = segment.parse::<usize>() {
937:         if let Some(items) = schema.get("items")
938:             && (items.is_object() || items.is_boolean())
939:         {
940:             return resolve_subschema(root, items, rest, visited, depth + 1);
941:         }
942:         // Try prefixItems[i]
943:         if let Some(prefix_items) = schema.get("prefixItems").and_then(|p| p.as_array())
944:             && let Some(item_schema) = prefix_items.get(idx)
945:         {
946:             return resolve_subschema(root, item_schema, rest, visited, depth + 1);
947:         }
948:     }
949: 
950:     None
951: }
952: 
953: /// Follow `$ref` within the same document. Only fragment references (`#/...`)
954: /// are followed. Returns the resolved schema, or the input schema if no `$ref`.
955: fn follow_ref<'a>(
956:     root: &'a serde_json::Value,
957:     schema: &'a serde_json::Value,
958:     visited: &mut HashSet<String>,
959: ) -> Option<&'a serde_json::Value> {
960:     let ref_str = match schema.get("$ref").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {
961:         Some(r) => r,
962:         None => return Some(schema),
963:     };
964: 
965:     // Only follow fragment references within the same document.
966:     if !ref_str.starts_with('#') {
967:         return Some(schema);
968:     }
969: 
970:     // Cycle detection.
971:     if !visited.insert(ref_str.to_string()) {
972:         return None;
973:     }
974: 
975:     // Parse the JSON Pointer from the fragment (strip leading `#/`).
976:     let pointer_str = ref_str.strip_prefix("#/").unwrap_or("");
977:     if pointer_str.is_empty() {
978:         return Some(root);
979:     }
980: 
981:     let mut target = root;
982:     for segment in pointer_str.split('/') {
983:         // Unescape JSON Pointer encoding (~1 = /, ~0 = ~).
984:         let unescaped = segment.replace("~1", "/").replace("~0", "~");
985:         target = target.get(&unescaped)?;
986:     }
987: 
988:     // Recursively follow if the target itself has a $ref.
989:     follow_ref(root, target, visited)
990: }

992: #[cfg(test)]
993: mod tests {
994:     use super::*;
995:     use std::io::Write;
996: 
997:     #[test]
998:     fn evict_forces_recompilation_from_disk() {
999:         let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();
1000:         let schema_path = dir.path().join("schema.json");
1001: 
1002:         // Write a schema that requires {"name": string}.
1003:         std::fs::write(
1004:             &schema_path,
1005:             r#"{"type":"object","properties":{"name":{"type":"string"}},"required":["name"]}"#,
1006:         )
1007:         .unwrap();
1008: 
1009:         let cache = SchemaCache::new();
1010:         let source = SchemaSource::File(schema_path.clone());
1011: 
1012:         // First compile should succeed and cache the validator.
1013:         let (validator_v1, _, _) = cache.get_or_compile(&source, true).unwrap();
1014: 
1015:         // Valid doc passes.
1016:         let doc: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(r#"{"name":"alice"}"#).unwrap();
1017:         assert!(validator_v1.is_valid(&doc));
1018: 
1019:         // Now overwrite the schema to require {"count": number} instead.
1020:         let mut f = std::fs::File::create(&schema_path).unwrap();
1021:         f.write_all(
1022:             br#"{"type":"object","properties":{"count":{"type":"number"}},"required":["count"]}"#,
1023:         )
1024:         .unwrap();
1025:         drop(f);
1026: 
1027:         // Without eviction, the cache still returns the old validator.
1028:         let (validator_stale, _, _) = cache.get_or_compile(&source, true).unwrap();
1029:         assert!(
1030:             validator_stale.is_valid(&doc),
1031:             "stale validator should still accept old doc"
1032:         );
1033: 
1034:         // Evict and recompile — now the new schema should be used.
1035:         assert!(cache.evict(&source));
1036:         let (validator_v2, _, _) = cache.get_or_compile(&source, true).unwrap();
1037: 
1038:         // Old doc is now invalid (missing "count").
1039:         assert!(!validator_v2.is_valid(&doc));
1040: 
1041:         // New doc passes.
1042:         let new_doc: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(r#"{"count":42}"#).unwrap();
1043:         assert!(validator_v2.is_valid(&new_doc));
1044:     }
1045:

1048:         let cache = SchemaCache::new();
1049:         let source = SchemaSource::File(PathBuf::from("/nonexistent/schema.json"));
1050:         assert!(!cache.evict(&source));
1051:     }

63: /// LSP server backend.
64: #[derive(Clone)]
65: pub struct Backend {
66:     client: Client,
67:     /// Open documents: URI → per-document state.
68:     documents: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<Uri, DocumentState>>>,
69:     /// jvl.json config cache: config file path → compiled config
70:     config_cache: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PathBuf, Arc<CompiledConfig>>>>,
71:     /// Compiled JSON Schema validator cache (shared with validate_file)
72:     schema_cache: Arc<SchemaCache>,
73:     /// Caps concurrent spawn_blocking validations to prevent thread pool pressure
74:     validation_semaphore: Arc<Semaphore>,
75:     /// True if the client negotiated UTF-8 position encoding; false = UTF-16 (default).
76:     utf8_positions: Arc<AtomicBool>,
77:     /// Schema file paths for which we have registered file watchers.
78:     watched_schema_paths: Arc<Mutex<HashSet<PathBuf>>>,
79:     /// Monotonically increasing counter for unique watcher registration IDs.
80:     next_reg_id: Arc<AtomicU64>,
81:     /// True if the client supports Markdown in hover content.
82:     hover_markdown: Arc<AtomicBool>,
83:     /// True if the client supports snippet syntax in completion insertText.
84:     snippet_support: Arc<AtomicBool>,
85: }

89:         f.debug_struct("Backend").finish()
90:     }
91: }
92: 
93: impl Backend {
94:     pub fn new(client: Client) -> Self {
95:         Self {
96:             client,
97:             documents: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
98:             config_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
99:             schema_cache: Arc::new(SchemaCache::new()),
100:             validation_semaphore: Arc::new(Semaphore::new(8)),
101:             utf8_positions: Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false)),
102:             watched_schema_paths: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
103:             next_reg_id: Arc::new(AtomicU64::new(0)),
104:             hover_markdown: Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(true)),
105:             snippet_support: Arc::new(AtomicBool::new(false)),
106:         }
107:     }
108: 
109:     /// Fire-and-forget task: debounce 200ms, validate, publish diagnostics.
110:     ///
111:     /// Captures `spawn_version` at spawn time so that — after the debounce sleep —
112:     /// the task can detect whether a newer edit arrived and self-cancel. This prevents
113:     /// multiple concurrent tasks (spawned by rapid edits) from all publishing diagnostics
114:     /// after the debounce window expires.
115:     fn spawn_validation(&self, uri: Uri) {
116:         // Capture the version at spawn time.  If a newer edit arrives before this task
117:         // wakes up, `current_version` will differ from `spawn_version` and the task discards.
118:         let spawn_version = {
119:             let docs = self.documents.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
120:             match docs.get(&uri) {
121:                 Some(state) => state.version,
122:                 None => return,
123:             }
124:         };
125: 
126:         // Clone self cheaply (all fields are Arc).
127:         let this = self.clone();
128: 
129:         // Detach the task; it runs until completion unless superseded by `spawn_version` check.
130:         tokio::spawn(async move {
131:             this.validate_and_publish(uri, spawn_version).await;
132:         });
133:     }
134: 
135:     /// Debounced validation task. Sleeps 200ms, snapshots content+version atomically,
136:     /// validates in `spawn_blocking`, then publishes diagnostics if the version is current.
137:     ///
138:     /// `spawn_version` is the document version at the time this task was spawned.
139:     /// After sleeping, the task compares the current version to `spawn_version`; if they
140:     /// differ, a newer edit superseded this task and it self-cancels.
141:     async fn validate_and_publish(&self, uri: Uri, spawn_version: i32) {
142:         // 1. Debounce: wait for typing to settle.
143:         tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200)).await;
144: 
145:         // 2. Limit concurrent blocking validations.
146:         let Ok(_permit) = self.validation_semaphore.acquire().await else {
147:             return;
148:         };
149: 
150:         // 3. Snapshot content + version together in ONE lock acquisition AFTER the sleep.
151:         //    This is critical — capturing content at didChange time can produce stale
152:         //    content that passes the version guard (the debounce race condition).
153:         let snapshot = {
154:             let docs = self.documents.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
155:             docs.get(&uri)
156:                 .map(|state| (state.version, state.content.clone()))
157:         };
158:         let Some((current_version, content)) = snapshot else {
159:             return; // Document was closed during the debounce window.
160:         };
161: 
162:         // 4. Check whether this task is still the right one.
163:         //    If the current version doesn't match the spawn-time version, a newer
164:         //    edit arrived while we were sleeping — another task handles that version.
165:         if current_version != spawn_version {
166:             return;
167:         }
168: 
169:         let version = current_version;
170: 
171:         // 5. Resolve file path (non-file URIs were already filtered in did_open).
172:         let Some(file_path) = uri.to_file_path().map(Cow::into_owned) else {
173:             return;
174:         };
175: 
176:         // 6. All blocking I/O (config resolution + validation) in spawn_blocking.
177:         let path_str = file_path.display().to_string();
178:         let schema_cache_clone = Arc::clone(&self.schema_cache);
179:         let config_cache_clone = Arc::clone(&self.config_cache);
180:         let content_clone = content.clone();
181:         let file_path_clone = file_path.clone();
182: 
183:         let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
184:             // Try to parse for the stale value cache (cheap relative to validation).
185:             let parsed_value = parse::parse_jsonc(&content_clone)
186:                 .ok()
187:                 .map(|p| Arc::new(p.value));
188: 
189:             let resolved = resolve_schema_for_document(&file_path_clone, &config_cache_clone);
190: 
191:             let validate_result = validate::validate_file(
192:                 &path_str,
193:                 &content_clone,
194:                 resolved.schema_source.as_ref(),
195:                 &schema_cache_clone,
196:                 false, // no_cache: always use disk cache in LSP mode
197:                 resolved.strict,
198:             );
199: 
200:             (validate_result, resolved.config_log, parsed_value)
201:         })
202:         .await;
203: 
204:         let ((file_result, warnings, _, _), config_log, parsed_value) = match result {
205:             Ok(r) => r,
206:             Err(e) => {
207:                 self.client
208:                     .log_message(
209:                         MessageType::ERROR,
210:                         format!("jvl: validation task panicked: {e}"),
211:                     )
212:                     .await;
213:                 return;
214:             }
215:         };
216: 
217:         // Log config errors and cache warnings to the editor output panel.
218:         if let Some(msg) = config_log {
219:             self.client.log_message(MessageType::WARNING, msg).await;
220:         }
221:         for warning in &warnings {
222:             self.client
223:                 .log_message(MessageType::WARNING, &warning.message)
224:                 .await;
225:         }
226: 
227:         // 7. Post-validation version guard + update stale value cache.
228:         //    Done in a single lock acquisition to avoid a TOCTOU race where a new
229:         //    edit could arrive between the version check and the cache update.
230:         {
231:             let mut docs = self.documents.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
232:             let still_current = docs
233:                 .get(&uri)
234:                 .map(|state| state.version == version)
235:                 .unwrap_or(false);
236:             if !still_current {
237:                 return;
238:             }
239:             if let Some(value) = parsed_value
240:                 && let Some(state) = docs.get_mut(&uri)
241:             {
242:                 state.last_good_value = Some(value);
243:             }
244:         }
245: 
246:         // 9. Convert and publish diagnostics.
247:         //    Compute line starts once for the full document, then pass to each converter.
248:         let utf8 = self.utf8_positions.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
249:         let line_starts = parse::compute_line_starts(&content);
250:         let diagnostics: Vec<Diagnostic> = file_result
251:             .errors
252:             .iter()
253:             .map(|d| file_diagnostic_to_lsp(d, &content, &line_starts, utf8))
254:             .collect();
255: 
256:         self.client
257:             .publish_diagnostics(uri, diagnostics, None)
258:             .await;
259: 
260:         // Register file watchers for any newly discovered schema files.
261:         self.update_schema_watchers().await;
262:     }
263: 
264:     /// Snapshot the current document text for the given URI.
265:     fn snapshot_document(&self, uri: &Uri) -> Option<Arc<String>> {
266:         let docs = self.documents.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
267:         docs.get(uri).map(|state| state.content.clone())
268:     }
269: 
270:     /// Resolve the schema source for a document URI, compile it, and return
271:     /// the raw schema `serde_json::Value`. Returns `None` if the URI is not a
272:     /// file:// URI, no schema is configured, or the schema fails to compile.
273:     fn resolve_schema_value(
274:         &self,
275:         uri: &Uri,
276:         parsed_value: &serde_json::Value,
277:     ) -> Option<Arc<serde_json::Value>> {
278:         let file_path = uri.to_file_path().map(Cow::into_owned)?;
279:         let resolved = resolve_schema_for_document(&file_path, &self.config_cache);
280: 
281:         let schema_source = resolved
282:             .schema_source
283:             .or_else(|| schema::resolve_schema_from_value(parsed_value, &file_path))?;
284: 
285:         match self
286:             .schema_cache
287:             .get_or_compile_with_value(&schema_source, false)
288:         {
289:             Ok(Some(v)) => Some(v),
290:             _ => None,
291:         }
292:     }
293: 
294:     /// Register file watchers for newly discovered schema file paths.
295:     ///
296:     /// Queries the schema cache for all `SchemaSource::File` entries and
297:     /// registers a watcher for each path not already being watched.
298:     async fn update_schema_watchers(&self) {
299:         let new_paths: Vec<PathBuf> = {
300:             let mut watched = self
301:                 .watched_schema_paths
302:                 .lock()
303:                 .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
304:             self.schema_cache
305:                 .cached_file_paths()
306:                 .into_iter()
307:                 .filter(|p| watched.insert(p.clone()))
308:                 .collect()
309:         };
310: 
311:         if new_paths.is_empty() {
312:             return;
313:         }
314: 
315:         let watchers: Vec<FileSystemWatcher> = new_paths
316:             .iter()
317:             .map(|p| FileSystemWatcher {
318:                 glob_pattern: GlobPattern::String(escape_glob_metacharacters(
319:                     &p.display().to_string(),
320:                 )),
321:                 kind: Some(WatchKind::Create | WatchKind::Change | WatchKind::Delete),
322:             })
323:             .collect();
324: 
325:         let reg_id = self.next_reg_id.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
326:         let registration = Registration {
327:             id: format!("jvl-schema-watch-{reg_id}"),
328:             method: "workspace/didChangeWatchedFiles".to_string(),
329:             register_options: Some(
330:                 serde_json::to_value(DidChangeWatchedFilesRegistrationOptions { watchers })
331:                     .unwrap(),
332:             ),
333:         };
334: 
335:         if let Err(e) = self.client.register_capability(vec![registration]).await {
336:             self.client
337:                 .log_message(
338:                     MessageType::WARNING,
339:                     format!("jvl: failed to register schema file watcher ({e})"),
340:                 )
341:                 .await;
342:         }
343:     }
344: }

346: impl LanguageServer for Backend {
347:     async fn initialize(&self, params: InitializeParams) -> Result<InitializeResult> {
348:         // Negotiate positionEncoding: prefer UTF-8 if the client advertises it.
349:         let utf8 = params
350:             .capabilities
351:             .general
352:             .as_ref()
353:             .and_then(|g| g.position_encodings.as_ref())
354:             .and_then(|encs| {
355:                 encs.iter()
356:                     .find(|e| e.as_str() == PositionEncodingKind::UTF8.as_str())
357:             })
358:             .is_some();
359: 
360:         self.utf8_positions.store(utf8, Ordering::Relaxed);
361: 
362:         // Check if client supports Markdown in hover content.
363:         if let Some(text_doc) = &params.capabilities.text_document
364:             && let Some(hover_caps) = &text_doc.hover
365:             && let Some(formats) = &hover_caps.content_format
366:         {
367:             self.hover_markdown
368:                 .store(formats.contains(&MarkupKind::Markdown), Ordering::Relaxed);
369:         }
370: 
371:         // Check if client supports snippet syntax in completion insertText.
372:         if let Some(text_doc) = &params.capabilities.text_document
373:             && let Some(completion_caps) = &text_doc.completion
374:             && let Some(item_caps) = &completion_caps.completion_item
375:             && item_caps.snippet_support == Some(true)
376:         {
377:             self.snippet_support.store(true, Ordering::Relaxed);
378:         }
379: 
380:         let position_encoding = if utf8 {
381:             PositionEncodingKind::UTF8
382:         } else {
383:             PositionEncodingKind::UTF16
384:         };
385: 
386:         Ok(InitializeResult {
387:             server_info: Some(ServerInfo {
388:                 name: "jvl".to_string(),
389:                 version: Some(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION").to_string()),
390:             }),
391:             capabilities: ServerCapabilities {
392:                 position_encoding: Some(position_encoding),
393:                 text_document_sync: Some(TextDocumentSyncCapability::Kind(
394:                     TextDocumentSyncKind::FULL,
395:                 )),
396:                 hover_provider: Some(HoverProviderCapability::Simple(true)),
397:                 completion_provider: Some(CompletionOptions {
398:                     trigger_characters: Some(vec!["\"".to_string()]),
399:                     resolve_provider: Some(false),
400:                     ..Default::default()
401:                 }),
402:                 ..Default::default()
403:             },
404:         })
405:     }
406: 
407:     async fn initialized(&self, _: InitializedParams) {
408:         // Register a file watcher for jvl.json so we invalidate the config
409:         // cache when the user edits their project config.
410:         let registration = Registration {
411:             id: "jvl-config-watch".to_string(),
412:             method: "workspace/didChangeWatchedFiles".to_string(),
413:             register_options: Some(
414:                 serde_json::to_value(DidChangeWatchedFilesRegistrationOptions {
415:                     watchers: vec![FileSystemWatcher {
416:                         glob_pattern: GlobPattern::String("**/jvl.json".to_string()),
417:                         kind: Some(WatchKind::Create | WatchKind::Change | WatchKind::Delete),
418:                     }],
419:                 })
420:                 .unwrap(),
421:             ),
422:         };
423: 
424:         if let Err(e) = self.client.register_capability(vec![registration]).await {
425:             self.client
426:                 .log_message(
427:                     MessageType::WARNING,
428:                     format!(
429:                         "jvl: failed to register file watcher ({e}); \
430:                          config changes won't trigger re-validation"
431:                     ),
432:                 )
433:                 .await;
434:         }
435:     }
436: 
437:     async fn shutdown(&self) -> Result<()> {
438:         Ok(())
439:     }
440: 
441:     async fn did_open(&self, params: DidOpenTextDocumentParams) {
442:         let uri = params.text_document.uri;
443:         let version = params.text_document.version;
444:         let content = params.text_document.text;
445: 
446:         // Only handle file:// URIs; log and skip others.
447:         if uri.to_file_path().is_none() {
448:             self.client
449:                 .log_message(
450:                     MessageType::INFO,
451:                     format!("jvl: skipping non-file URI: {}", uri.as_str()),
452:                 )
453:                 .await;
454:             return;
455:         }
456: 
457:         {
458:             let mut docs = self.documents.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
459:             docs.insert(
460:                 uri.clone(),
461:                 DocumentState {
462:                     version,
463:                     content: Arc::new(content),
464:                     last_good_value: None,
465:                 },
466:             );
467:         }
468: 
469:         self.spawn_validation(uri);
470:     }
471: 
472:     async fn did_change(&self, params: DidChangeTextDocumentParams) {
473:         let uri = params.text_document.uri;
474:         let version = params.text_document.version;
475: 
476:         // FULL sync: take the first (and only) content change.
477:         let Some(content) = params.content_changes.into_iter().next().map(|c| c.text) else {
478:             return;
479:         };
480: 
481:         {
482:             let mut docs = self.documents.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
483:             if let Some(state) = docs.get_mut(&uri) {
484:                 state.version = version;
485:                 state.content = Arc::new(content);
486:             }
487:         }
488: 
489:         self.spawn_validation(uri);
490:     }
491: 
492:     async fn did_close(&self, params: DidCloseTextDocumentParams) {
493:         let uri = params.text_document.uri;
494: 
495:         // Remove from document map so any in-flight validation discards its result.
496:         self.documents
497:             .lock()
498:             .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
499:             .remove(&uri);
500: 
501:         // Clear diagnostics for this document.
502:         self.client.publish_diagnostics(uri, vec![], None).await;
503:     }
504: 
505:     async fn hover(&self, params: HoverParams) -> Result<Option<Hover>> {
506:         let uri = params.text_document_position_params.text_document.uri;
507:         let position = params.text_document_position_params.position;
508: 
509:         // 1. Snapshot document text.
510:         let Some(content) = self.snapshot_document(&uri) else {
511:             return Ok(None);
512:         };
513: 
514:         // 2. Parse the document.
515:         let parsed = match parse::parse_jsonc(&content) {
516:             Ok(p) => p,
517:             Err(_) => return Ok(None),
518:         };
519: 
520:         // 3. Convert LSP position to byte offset.
521:         let utf8 = self.utf8_positions.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
522:         let line_starts = parse::compute_line_starts(&content);
523:         let Some(byte_offset) = lsp_position_to_byte_offset(&content, &line_starts, position, utf8)
524:         else {
525:             return Ok(None);
526:         };
527: 
528:         // 4. Find JSON pointer at byte offset.
529:         let Some((pointer, node_range)) = parse::offset_to_pointer(&parsed.ast, byte_offset) else {
530:             return Ok(None);
531:         };
532: 
533:         // 5. Resolve schema value for this document.
534:         let Some(schema_value) = self.resolve_schema_value(&uri, &parsed.value) else {
535:             return Ok(None);
536:         };
537: 
538:         // 6. Look up hover content from schema annotations.
539:         let Some(hover_content) = schema::lookup_hover_content(&schema_value, &pointer) else {
540:             return Ok(None);
541:         };
542: 
543:         // 7. Convert node_range back to LSP Range.
544:         let start_pos = byte_offset_to_lsp_position(&content, &line_starts, node_range.start, utf8);
545:         let end_pos = byte_offset_to_lsp_position(&content, &line_starts, node_range.end, utf8);
546: 
547:         let kind = if self.hover_markdown.load(Ordering::Relaxed) {
548:             MarkupKind::Markdown
549:         } else {
550:             MarkupKind::PlainText
551:         };
552: 
553:         Ok(Some(Hover {
554:             contents: HoverContents::Markup(MarkupContent {
555:                 kind,
556:                 value: hover_content,
557:             }),
558:             range: Some(Range::new(start_pos, end_pos)),
559:         }))
560:     }
561: 
562:     async fn completion(&self, params: CompletionParams) -> Result<Option<CompletionResponse>> {
563:         let uri = params.text_document_position.text_document.uri;
564:         let position = params.text_document_position.position;
565: 
566:         // 1. Snapshot document text.
567:         let Some(content) = self.snapshot_document(&uri) else {
568:             return Ok(None);
569:         };
570: 
571:         // 2. Try parsing current text; fall back to stale cached value.
572:         let parsed_value = match parse::parse_jsonc(&content) {
573:             Ok(p) => Arc::new(p.value),
574:             Err(_) => {
575:                 let stale = self
576:                     .documents
577:                     .lock()
578:                     .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
579:                     .get(&uri)
580:                     .and_then(|state| state.last_good_value.clone());
581:                 match stale {
582:                     Some(v) => v,
583:                     None => return Ok(None),
584:                 }
585:             }
586:         };
587: 
588:         // 3. Convert LSP position to byte offset.
589:         let utf8 = self.utf8_positions.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
590:         let line_starts = parse::compute_line_starts(&content);
591:         let Some(byte_offset) = lsp_position_to_byte_offset(&content, &line_starts, position, utf8)
592:         else {
593:             return Ok(None);
594:         };
595: 
596:         // 4. Determine completion context via text scanning.
597:         let Some(ctx) = parse::completion_context(&content, byte_offset) else {
598:             return Ok(None);
599:         };
600: 
601:         // 5. Resolve schema value for this document.
602:         let Some(schema_value) = self.resolve_schema_value(&uri, &parsed_value) else {
603:             return Ok(None);
604:         };
605: 
606:         // 6. Compute the replacement range for text_edit.
607:         //    replace_start comes from completion_context (opening quote if inside
608:         //    a string, otherwise the cursor position).  replace_end extends past
609:         //    a closing quote at the cursor when the editor auto-paired it.
610:         let replace_start = match &ctx {
611:             parse::CompletionContext::PropertyKey { replace_start, .. }
612:             | parse::CompletionContext::PropertyValue { replace_start, .. } => *replace_start,
613:         };
614:         let replace_end = if content.as_bytes().get(byte_offset) == Some(&b'"') {
615:             byte_offset + 1
616:         } else {
617:             byte_offset
618:         };
619:         let replace_range = Range::new(
620:             byte_offset_to_lsp_position(&content, &line_starts, replace_start, utf8),
621:             byte_offset_to_lsp_position(&content, &line_starts, replace_end, utf8),
622:         );
623: 
624:         // 7. Build completion items based on context.
625:         let snippet = self.snippet_support.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
626:         let markdown = self.hover_markdown.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
627: 
628:         let items = match &ctx {
629:             parse::CompletionContext::PropertyKey { pointer, .. } => {
630:                 let props = schema::collect_properties(&schema_value, pointer);
631:                 let existing = existing_keys(&parsed_value, pointer);
632:                 build_property_items(&props, &existing, snippet, markdown, replace_range)
633:             }
634:             parse::CompletionContext::PropertyValue {
635:                 property_name,
636:                 pointer,
637:                 ..
638:             } => {
639:                 let values = schema::collect_values(&schema_value, pointer, property_name);
640:                 build_value_items(&values, replace_range)
641:             }
642:         };
643: 
644:         Ok(Some(CompletionResponse::List(CompletionList {
645:             is_incomplete: false,
646:             items,
647:         })))
648:     }
649: 
650:     async fn did_change_watched_files(&self, params: DidChangeWatchedFilesParams) {
651:         let changed: Vec<PathBuf> = params
652:             .changes
653:             .iter()
654:             .filter_map(|c| c.uri.to_file_path().map(Cow::into_owned))
655:             .collect();
656: 
657:         // Evict config cache for jvl.json changes.
658:         let mut config_changed = false;
659:         {
660:             let mut cache = self.config_cache.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
661:             for path in &changed {
662:                 if path.file_name() == Some(std::ffi::OsStr::new("jvl.json")) {
663:                     cache.remove(path);
664:                     config_changed = true;
665:                 }
666:             }
667:         }
668: 
669:         // On config change, clear watched schema paths so they are
670:         // rediscovered during re-validation.
671:         if config_changed {
672:             self.watched_schema_paths
673:                 .lock()
674:                 .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
675:                 .clear();
676:         }
677: 
678:         // Evict schema cache for changed files.
679:         let mut schema_changed = false;
680:         for path in &changed {
681:             if self.schema_cache.evict(&SchemaSource::file(path.clone())) {
682:                 schema_changed = true;
683:             }
684:         }
685: 
686:         // Only re-validate if something was actually invalidated.
687:         if config_changed || schema_changed {
688:             let uris: Vec<Uri> = self
689:                 .documents
690:                 .lock()
691:                 .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
692:                 .keys()
693:                 .cloned()
694:                 .collect();
695: 
696:             for uri in uris {
697:                 self.spawn_validation(uri);
698:             }
699:         }
700:     }
701: }
